tldr: Mongo Atlas to CockroachDB is a database paradigm change, not just a vendor swap: document → distributed SQL. The migration requires data modeling work, not just a dump-and-load.
why_switch: Standard SQL with PostgreSQL wire compatibility
Distributed serializable transactions out of the box
Stronger consistency model for financial/transactional apps
what_to_export: Network access lists and IP allowlists
export_method: Use mongodump to produce a BSON archive of every database, including indexes. For very large datasets, use the Atlas live-migration tooling or take a cluster snapshot, restore it to a temporary cluster, and dump from there to avoid load on production. Export Atlas Search index definitions and any aggregation pipelines as code so you have the logic captured. Pull users, roles, and network access via the Atlas Admin API. Keep the BSON archives plus a JSON inventory of indexes and validators in object storage as the durable handoff artifact.
import_method: CockroachDB does not accept MongoDB BSON directly — the document model must be remapped to relational SQL. Design target schemas: choose between relational tables for structured fields and JSONB columns for semi-structured payloads. Convert mongodump output to CSV/Avro/SQL via a transform script (or use a tool like Debezium for CDC-based migration). Load into CockroachDB using IMPORT INTO from cloud storage. Recreate indexes and constraints. Aggregation pipelines must be rewritten as SQL queries (often window functions or CTEs). Application code that uses MongoDB drivers must switch to a Postgres driver/ORM. Run dual-write or shadow reads for 1-2 weeks before cutover to validate parity.

concept_mapping.note: Choose relational vs. JSONB per field

gotchas: Document → relational remap is the real cost; budget engineering time, not just ops time
Atlas Search has no first-party equivalent — plan a separate search service if used heavily
Aggregation pipelines must be rewritten in SQL, including any nested or array operators
Dedicated Atlas cluster tiers don't scale down mid-cycle; plan cancellation around the billing boundary
Application code uses MongoDB drivers — every service must move to a Postgres driver/ORM
Run dual-write or shadow reads to validate parity before cutover; surprise mismatches are common

email.subject: Data export request and account sunset — 14-day timeline
email.body:
Hi MongoDB Atlas team,

We are migrating off Atlas to CockroachDB and need a complete export of our data in machine-readable form. Required: full mongodump archives per database (BSON or JSON), all index definitions, schema validators, Atlas Search index configs, users and roles, network access lists, backup snapshot inventory, and triggers/scheduled functions.

We are targeting a 14-day window for export and migration validation. Please confirm:

1. Which exports are self-serve via mongodump/Admin API vs. require support.
2. Whether snapshot restores can be staged to a short-lived cluster for offline dump without impacting production.
3. Billing implications of pausing or downsizing dedicated clusters during dual-run, and the exact account sunset date once we cancel.

Thanks,
Your customer
